name: setup_app_env
description: Set up the application environment: start PostgreSQL if needed, create database user and database, load environment variables from example.env. Bundles everything needed to get the app running.
when_to_use: Use when the task requires running the application or database tests. Replaces multiple steps: checking PostgreSQL status, starting it, creating user/database, loading env vars.

examples.expected: PostgreSQL started, user 'myuser' created, database 'simplelogin' created, env vars loaded from example.env.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 5-7 separate bash commands (pg_lsclusters, pg_ctlcluster, CREATE USER, CREATE DATABASE, CREATE EXTENSION, loading env vars) with a single script call. Cuts both round trips and output token volume.
